data.title: Freeport to delay Indonesian copper sales to Q2 2025 after fire - Reuters
data.description:
Freeport McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) will postpone sales of refined copper from Indonesia until Q2 2025, as a fire at its new Manyar smelter causes a further production delay, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Freeport (FCX) said earlier it is investigating the cause of the fire at a sulfuric acid unit at Manyar, with a production capacity of 480K metric tons/year of copper cathode, which was extinguished on Monday.

Analysts say long production delays at the new smelter likely will narrow an expected 2025 copper surplus and support prices.

Also, Freeport (FCX) has been in talks with the Indonesian government to extend its export license for copper concentrates which expires at year-end 2024 into next year's Q1, according to the report.

data.title: Rapid7, Tenable, Qualys in focus as Jefferies starts coverage on trio
data.description:
Rapid7 (NASDAQ:RPD), Tenable (NASDAQ:TENB) and Qualys (NASDAQ:QLYS) were in focus on Wednesday as investment firm Jefferies started coverage on the exposure management portion of the cybersecurity market.

Shares of Rapid7 were higher in premarket trading, while Tenable and Qualys were lower.

Rapid7

Rapid7 was started with a Buy rating and $50 price target as the company is going after the $30B exposure management space, a market that is expected to grow at a 16% compound annual rate through 2028, Jefferies analyst Joseph Gallo wrote in a research note.

Boston-based Rapid7 has gained traction in the security information and event management and cloud risk management areas, both of which are seen as non-core, Gallo said. This is important, given the company's core vulnerability management solutions are facing lower demand and macro pressures, he added.

"We see upside from improved execution with [go-to-market] changes & focus on the channel, all while the business trades at less than our implied base value," Gallo wrote.

Tenable

Gallo started coverage on Tenable with a Hold rating and $45 price target, noting it is the "most established player" in the exposure management space.

"Tenable One, its unified platform, presents an opportunity to offset cyclical and moderating core VM revenues by bundling additional solutions," Gallo wrote. "However, TENB is in early stages of the evolution beyond core [vulnerability management] (estimate 70% of the business today) making [valuation] feel fair."

Qualys

Qualys was started with a Hold rating and a $135 price target, as Gallo said that upside to growth is likely to remain "difficult" until its adjacent areas of growth play a bigger part for the overall company.

"QLYS is focused on investing in S&M, but we expect limited upside, especially given that it has the second-toughest new business (billings) set-up in our broader cyber coverage for CY25," Gallo wrote.

data.title: NOWDiagnostics and Labcorp collaborate to expand access to syphilis test
data.description:
• NOWDiagnostics said Labcorp (NYSE:LH) will be the exclusive U.S. distributor of its First To Know Syphilis Test in professional and healthcare settings.
  • The firm said helped by Labcorp’s (LH) distribution network, the collaboration will expand access to the syphilis test.
  • LH also plans to offer the test to professionals and hospital settings by the end of 2024 and directly to patients through Labcorp OnDemand in 2025.

data.title: INTERFOR to exit Québec operations
data.description:
• Canada-based wood products company INTERFOR (TSX:IFP:CA) said on Wednesday it plans to exit its operations in Québec.
  • The exit plan includes a sale of three manufacturing facilities and the closure of the Montréal corporate office.
  • The company said it intends to focus on areas with the highest future potential.
  • INTERFOR is set to sell its sawmills in Val-d'Or and Matagami as well as its Sullivan remanufacturing plant in Val-d'Or, along with the associated forestry and business operations, to the Québec-based forestry company Chantiers Chibougamau for ~$30M in cash.
  • The company anticipates an associated impairment charge in Q3.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Morgan Stanley in charts: Institutional Securities Q3 revenues jump 20% Y/Y
Keros Therapeutics names Yung Chyung as chief medical officer
data.description:
• Keros Therapeutics (NASDAQ:KROS) said it has appointed Yung Chyung as its chief medical officer, effective as of November 1.
  • Chyung had recently served as the chief medical officer and executive vice president of Tourmaline Bio.

data.title: Airbus will cut as many as 2,500 jobs from defense, space unit
data.description:
• Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) on Wednesday said it will cut as many as 2,500 jobs in its Defense and Space division. The European company pointed to a "complex business environment" including losses on satellites.
  • The planned cuts make up 7% of the workforce in its second-largest division. Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) intends to complete them by the middle of 2026 after talks with unions.

data.title: Elon Musk's Tesla transfers $760M Bitcoins to other wallets - report
data.description:
Elon Musk's Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has reportedly transferred almost all $760 million of Bitcoins (BTC-USD) that it owns to unidentified wallets.

The move has given rise to speculations that the electric carmaker might be selling the cryptocurrency.

Wallets which are associated with Tesla (TSLA) moved more than 11.5K BTC to other wallets, whose ownership has not been revealed yet, a CoinDesk report stated, citing data from Arkham Intelligence.

The Tesla (TSLA) wallets now only have about $6.65 worth of BTC, the report added.

The carmaker is the fourth-largest holder of the cryptocurrency among publicly listed companies in the U.S., according to BitcoinTreasuries data.

Earlier in March, a Morgan Stanley Research report suggested that since September last year, the historically positive correlation between Tesla’s (TSLA) share price and Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has broken down.

In 2022, Tesla (TSLA) had disclosed that it has sold 75% of its position in the cryptocurrency worth $936M. However, this was not the only time the carmaker liquidated some of its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) stash. The electric car manufacturer had sold $300M of BTC in Q1 2021 for what looked to be a decent profit.

BTC is currently trading at $68.2K.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures rise after Wall Street closes lower on tech sell off
data.description:
Stock index futures traded in the green, a day after chip stocks slumped on a disappointing outlook from ASML (ASML).

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.93%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4% in premarket trade, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

Morgan Stanley (MS) delivered a beat on both its top and bottom line in its third quarter, as banks continued to shine.

On the economic side, the September import/export prices and treasury buyback results are expected. The export prices index is expected to come in at -0.4%, while the import at -0.3% on a monthly basis.
